title: Community Relations: Conduct on School Premises
description: Conroe ISD sets clear expectations for how visitors and community members should behave while on school campuses and other district property. The policy helps ensure that schools remain safe, orderly environments where students can learn without disruption. Parents, volunteers, and other guests visiting district facilities are expected to follow these conduct standards at all times.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKA (Local) policy on Conduct on School Premises expands the tobacco prohibition in the "Tobacco and E-Cigarettes" section to explicitly include "electronic vaporizing devices," while removing the standalone word "and" that previously joined "tobacco products" and "e-cigarettes."
timeline_local.notes: The red underlined addition appears in the Tobacco and E-Cigarettes section, adding ", and electronic vaporizing devices" after "e-cigarettes." The red strikethrough removes the prior "and" that connected "tobacco products" and "e-cigarettes," replacing it with a comma-separated list structure. All other policy text (Access to District Property, Ejection or Exclusion, Off-Campus Activities, Weapons) is unchanged black text.

elements.element: Prohibition on tobacco products and e-cigarettes (core statutory mandate)
elements.statutory_basis: TEC §38.006(b)(1)–(2) as amended by Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 181 (S.B. 97), eff. October 1, 2015
elements.supporting_quote: The board of trustees of a school district shall: (1) prohibit smoking or using e-cigarettes or tobacco products at a school-related or school-sanctioned activity on or off school property; (2) prohibit students from possessing e-cigarettes or tobacco products at a school-related or school-sanctioned activity on or off school property
elements.local_additions: None — the policy's prohibition on tobacco products and e-cigarettes directly mirrors the statutory mandate. The grammatical restructuring (removal of standalone 'and') is an incidental drafting adjustment made necessary by the addition of the third term.

elements.element: Explicit extension of the prohibition to 'electronic vaporizing devices' as a distinct named category
elements.verification_method: web search confirmed — TEC §38.006 text confirmed unchanged as to enumerated device categories; no 89th Leg. amendment to §38.006 or THSC §161.081 adds 'electronic vaporizing devices' as a separate statutory term compelled in school board policy
elements.local_additions: The district chose to enumerate 'electronic vaporizing devices' as a third, standalone prohibited item beyond the two categories the statute names ('e-cigarettes' and 'tobacco products'). TEC §38.006 uses only the term 'e-cigarettes,' defined by reference to THSC §161.081. That definition may or may not encompass every device a layperson would call an 'electronic vaporizing device,' but the statute does not compel districts to use that specific term or to enumerate it separately. This is a locally chosen drafting expansion for policy clarity.

compliance_summary: Tobacco and E-Cigarette Ban: State law (TEC §38.006, updated by S.B. 97 in 2015) requires school districts to prohibit tobacco products and e-cigarettes on school property. This policy reflects that existing legal requirement.

Electronic Vaporizing Devices Added: The updated policy adds 'electronic vaporizing devices' as a separately named prohibited item, beyond the two categories the state law specifically mentions. This addition was a local drafting choice by the district, not a state requirement.

Grammatical Restructuring: The word 'and' that previously connected 'tobacco products' and 'e-cigarettes' was removed to accommodate the newly added third category. This was a minor drafting adjustment with no change in legal meaning.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Policy GKA (Local) expands the tobacco prohibition in the "Tobacco and E-Cigarettes" section to explicitly include "electronic vaporizing devices" alongside the existing prohibition on tobacco products and e-cigarettes on District property, in District vehicles, or at school-related activities.
timeline_local.notes: The red text in the Tobacco And E-Cigarettes section shows two changes: the word "and" (before "e-cigarettes") appears to be struck through in red and replaced, and the phrase "and electronic vaporizing devices" is added in red underline after "e-cigarettes." The resulting sentence reads: "...tobacco products, e-cigarettes, and electronic vaporizing devices..." The strikethrough of "and" is a minor grammatical restructuring to accommodate the new addition. Document is black-and-white scan so colour distinction is inferred from context and underlining/strikethrough formatting visible on page 3.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKA (Local) policy on Conduct on School Premises expands the tobacco prohibition under the "Tobacco And E-Cigarettes" section to explicitly include "electronic vaporizing devices" in addition to the previously listed e-cigarettes.
timeline_local.notes: The redline on page 3 shows the Tobacco And E-Cigarettes section with red underlined text adding ", and electronic vaporizing devices" after "e-cigarettes." The word "and" before "e-cigarettes" appears to have been changed (the old standalone "and" is struck through and replaced with a comma + "and electronic vaporizing devices" appended after "e-cigarettes"). The colour rendering in the PDF scan is somewhat ambiguous — the strikethrough on "and" is faint — but the addition of "and electronic vaporizing devices" is clearly underlined in red. Board action determined from cover page title "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy GKA (Local)" → first_reading.

description_local: This policy governs how members of the public are expected to behave when on school district property, including rules about prohibited items such as tobacco products and e-cigarettes. It applies to anyone who visits or spends time on school campuses or other district facilities, not just students and staff. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it sets clear expectations for conduct on school grounds and helps ensure a safe environment for everyone, particularly children.
